warhead_classes.name: Acrylamide
warhead_classes.notes: Most common covalent warhead in approved drugs. Good balance of reactivity and selectivity.

warhead_classes.name: Chloroacetamide
warhead_classes.notes: Higher reactivity than acrylamides. Used in chemical probes and early-stage drug discovery.

warhead_classes.name: Vinyl sulfonamide
warhead_classes.notes: Can target both cysteine and lysine residues. Tunable reactivity via substituents.

warhead_classes.name: Cyanoacrylamide
warhead_classes.notes: Reversible covalent warhead. Forms thiol adduct that can dissociate, reducing off-target toxicity.

warhead_classes.name: Propynamide
warhead_classes.notes: Low intrinsic reactivity provides high selectivity. Requires precise positioning.

warhead_classes.name: Alpha-beta unsaturated ketone
warhead_classes.notes: High reactivity can lead to off-target effects. Used when potency is prioritized.

warhead_classes.name: Sulfonyl fluoride
warhead_classes.notes: Can target multiple nucleophilic residues. Useful for proteome-wide profiling.

warhead_classes.name: Aryl sulfonyl fluoride
warhead_classes.notes: Click chemistry-derived warhead. Growing interest for covalent probe development.

warhead_classes.name: Epoxide
warhead_classes.notes: Potent but less selective. Used in proteasome inhibitors.

warhead_classes.name: Nitrile
warhead_classes.notes: Reversible covalent. Used in DPP-4 inhibitors and SARS-CoV-2 main protease inhibitors.

cysteine.druggability_notes: Most commonly targeted residue for covalent drugs. Low abundance increases selectivity potential.

lysine.druggability_notes: Emerging target for covalent drugs. Higher abundance makes selectivity challenging.

serine.druggability_notes: Targeted by sulfonyl fluorides and some electrophilic warheads.

tyrosine.druggability_notes: Can be targeted by SuFEx chemistry. Less explored than cysteine.
